steps.title: Separate actual and predicted ratings
steps.content: The prompt says to assume P6 gave equal actual ratings to abstract and cubist paintings. The question asks what the model predicted, so the graph's model-participant correlation data matter.

steps.title: Use the correlation difference
steps.content: Choices B and D make unsupported claims about which style P6 enjoyed more, and Choice C says the predicted ratings were equal. The graph indicates different model patterns for the two styles, so the predicted ratings differed. The answer is A.
